---
title: "Final Paper"
author: "Linda Herrera, Natalie Chen, June McNally"
format: 
  pdf:
    include-in-header: 
      text: |
        \usepackage{fvextra}
        \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
        \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{OutputCode}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \definecolor{coldblue}{RGB}{31, 73, 163}
        \definecolor{lightblue}{RGB}{166, 185, 226}
        \definecolor{lightyellow}{RGB}{255, 230, 150}
        \definecolor{softorange}{RGB}{240, 160, 90}
        \definecolor{hotred}{RGB}{196, 30, 38}
execute: 
  warning: false
  echo: false
  message: false
---

## Introduction

<!-- 
- Establish context for study:
    - Relevance to NCOS
    - Relevance to other ecosystems
- Synthesize main findings from other papers (connect ideas, don't just list)
- Articulate main question(s) for the study
-->

## Methods

<!--
- Describe temporal and spatial coverage of dataset
- Reference monitoring report or NCOS-related publications on data collection
- Describe frequency of data collection
- Describe statistical analysis or descriptive statistics (linked to your question)
-->

## Results

<!--
- Discuss findings clearly (patterns, trends, etc.)
- Connect results to your research questions and figures
- Present figures in an order that tells a clear ecological story
-->

## Discussion

<!--
- Connect results to NCOS reports or publications
- Connect results to other ecosystems
- Address challenges in data collection:
    - What would be difficult about collecting this data
    - What other variables influence the patterns you see, how they're collected, and why they're relevant
    - Temporal changes that may influence patterns, and how (or whether) your dataset captures them
-->

## References

<!--
- Use in-text citations consistently
- Reference section auto-generated from .bib file
- Ensure references are relevant to the claims being made
-->
